name: True Crime: New York City
post_apply_message:
True Crime: New York City (2005, Activision, delisted) — Apply adds the Steam shortcut on GE-Proton 10.34 with the d3d9 override.

THE KEY BIT — THE d3d9 WRAPPER: your copy already ships a d3d9.dll wrapper (its d3d9.ini is in the game root: FPS limiter + forced-windowed). Wine must load THAT dll, not its own DXVK d3d9 — so the shortcut carries WINEDLLOVERRIDES="d3d9=n,b". Without it the wrapper never loads and you get the vanilla (broken on modern setups) renderer.

WIDESCREEN / DISPLAY: the wrapper's d3d9.ini controls windowing and the 30 FPS cap. It's left as shipped on purpose — this game's physics/animation can misbehave above its intended frame cap, so raise FPSLimit only if you've confirmed it's safe. Edit d3d9.ini in the game folder if you want to tweak it.

CONTROLLER: native pad support — Steam Input's gamepad layout works.
notes: Built 2026-07-21 from Tony's copy staged at NAS _games/'True Crime - New York City'. The launch exe name literally contains a registered-trademark sign: 'True Crime® New York City.exe' (19.2MB) — kept verbatim in the shortcut. A d3d9.dll wrapper is bundled (d3d9.ini present: [MAIN] FPSLimit=30, ForceWindowedMode=1, [LAUNCHER] AppExe pointing at the game exe) — the whole fix hinges on Wine loading the local d3d9.dll, hence the WINEDLLOVERRIDES. detect marker is d3d9.ini (stable, unlike the ® exe name). Delisted/abandonware. Non-Steam -> computed appid. UNTESTED on hardware — first thing to confirm is that the d3d9 override actually loads the wrapper (PROTON_LOG grep d3d9 if the game looks wrong).
